input.code:
def connect(host, port, user):
    import subprocess
    cmd = f'ssh {user}@{host} -p {port}'
    subprocess.call(cmd, shell=True)
    return True

bug_descriptions: shell=True with f-string allows command injection via host/user/port
String interpolation in SQL query allows SQL injection

input.note: INTENTIONAL VULNERABILITY - benchmark test case for code review quality evaluation

bug_descriptions: Inserting unsanitized user input as HTML enables cross-site scripting
os.path.join with user input allows path traversal via ../
yaml.load without Loader parameter allows arbitrary code execution

input.code:
def download_file(url):
    conn = http.client.HTTPSConnection(url.split('/')[2])
    conn.request('GET', '/' + '/'.join(url.split('/')[3:]))
    response = conn.getresponse()
    data = response.read()
    return data

bug_descriptions: HTTP connection is never closed; response not properly handled
Loop starts at index 1, skipping comparison of first element pair; last element always excluded from results
API key and database password hardcoded in source code
Shared mutable counter without synchronization causes race condition
Errors from ReadAll and Unmarshal are ignored; type assertion without nil check causes panic
int() raises ValueError on non-numeric input; None return used in f-string without check
Admin endpoints have no authentication middleware; DELETE query uses string concatenation
